audits.summary: Identified critical signature verification and guardian set issues; most findings remediated before mainnet relaunch post-exploit.
Post-exploit audit confirming the signature verification fix and identifying residual lower-severity issues in Solana program logic.

audits.scope: Wormhole EVM token bridge and core contracts
audits.summary: Reviewed EVM-side bridge logic; flagged centralization risks in guardian multisig and upgrade key management.

audits.scope: Wormhole cross-chain messaging layer and relayer contracts
audits.summary: Assessed relayer and automatic delivery contracts; found one high-severity reentrancy-adjacent issue, subsequently fixed.
Clean review of upgraded Solana programs with no critical findings; minor informational issues noted.

audits.scope: Wormhole Gateway (Cosmos/IBC integration) and NTT (Native Token Transfers)
audits.summary: Reviewed Cosmos-side gateway and NTT framework; two high-severity logic errors in IBC packet handling were remediated.
Second Trail of Bits engagement covering NTT; one high-severity access control gap identified and fixed prior to launch.

exploit_history.description: Attacker exploited a missing account validation in the Solana core bridge's `verify_signatures` instruction, allowing minting of 120,000 wETH (~$320M) without depositing collateral. The guardian signature verification was bypassed via a deprecated `solana_program::sysvar::instructions` syscall that was not properly validated.

Wormhole-adjacent: a misconfigured relayer on a third-party integration caused stuck transactions and temporary DoS on one connected chain; no funds lost, resolved within hours. (Note: this is a minor operational incident, not a protocol exploit.)
exploit_history.post_mortem_url: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — no public post-mortem found on Rekt.news or DefiLlama for this minor incident

dependencies.notes: Wormhole uses its own guardian network of 19 nodes (operated by known validators like Jump, Everstake, Chorus One, etc.) rather than an external oracle. A 13/19 supermajority is required to sign VAAs. Compromise of 7+ guardians would be catastrophic; guardian identities are public but node infrastructure security is opaque.

All cross-chain messages depend on the core bridge contracts which were the site of the $320M 2022 exploit. Contracts have been re-audited but the fundamental trust model relies on guardian honesty and liveness.
EVM-side contracts inherit Ethereum L1 security; no additional risk beyond standard smart contract exposure.
Solana program runtime has historically had unique vulnerability classes (account confusion, sysvar misuse) that contributed to the 2022 exploit. Solana's BPF VM adds non-EVM-standard risk surface.
Wormhole supports 30+ chains including major L2s; sequencer centralization on individual L2s is a downstream risk but does not directly threaten Wormhole's core security model.

rationale: Wormhole carries significant historical risk given the catastrophic $320M February 2022 exploit — one of the largest DeFi hacks ever — which exposed a fundamental flaw in Solana program account validation; the protocol was made whole by Jump Crypto injecting capital, not by recovered funds. Post-exploit, Wormhole has substantially improved its security posture with 7+ audits from reputable firms, a $2.5M Immunefi bug bounty, and a 13/19 guardian multisig, but the trust model remains concentrated in a known set of 19 guardian nodes whose infrastructure security is not publicly verifiable. The combination of multi-chain complexity (30+ chains), a prior catastrophic exploit, and guardian-set centralization places Wormhole in a medium-high risk category despite its improved audit coverage.
